tags: [] Placeholder for BASW Continuing Professional Development reflection to be added in approximately 18 months.

item.title: Professional Recommendation
item.description:
tags: [] Placeholder for professional recommendation letter to be added in approximately 6 months.

This recommendation will come from a professional mentor in the social work field, detailing my practical skills and professional development.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Email:

ken@kenchege.com: responses typically provided within 24 hours.

Location

Nairobi, Kenya

Connect Professionally

I welcome connections from:

  • Social Work professionals
  • Academic colleagues
  • Potential collaborators
  • Students in the field

Please allow 2-3 working days for a response to non-urgent inquiries.

item.title: CV
item.description:
• E: ken@kenchege.com
  • Social Work Student
  • Coventry University

KEN CHEGE

PROFILE SUMMARY

A dedicated International Relations graduate transitioning into social work, with demonstrated commitment to community welfare and individual support. Combines academic rigor with practical experience in healthcare, pharmaceutical sales, and community service, showing strong empathy, resilience, and cross-cultural communication skills. Seeking to leverage this background in MA Social Work to serve vulnerable populations and contribute to humanitarian protection efforts.

TECHNICAL SKILLS

  • Project Management: Successfully completed the Project Management Professional Programme, where I learned motivational, adaptable, and strategic skills.
  • Interpersonal Communication: Utilized, maintained, and created new relationships with doctors and customers to boost sales as a pharmaceutical sales representative.
  • Policy and Advocacy Support: Ability to update the repository of policy documents, develop PowerPoint presentations and briefing documents, and support stakeholder engagement as an international relations student.
  • Content Development: Drafted reports, briefs, factsheets, and other documents to the manager in pharmaceutical sales.
  • Research, Writing, and Analytical Skills: Proven ability to deliver high quality outputs, communicate effectively in written forms, and conduct research and analysis on social protection topics.
  • Safety Skills: Knowledge of fire safety and first aid procedures.
  • Support Independence, Wellbeing and Facilitate Empowerment: Acquired through an aged care certificate.

Soft Skills

In addition to my technical skills, my power skills include collaborative leadership, empathy, communication, problem-solving, an innovative mindset, and for-purpose orientation.

item.title: Professional Introduction
item.description:
Professional Introduction

[Your Name] | [Your Contact Info]

Dear Hiring Committee/Recipient,

I am writing to introduce myself as a dedicated [Your Current Role/Student Status] with a passion for [Your Field]. Currently pursuing my Master of Social Work at [Your University], I bring [Number] years of experience in [Your Experience Areas].

Professional Philosophy

My approach to social work is grounded in [Key Principles]. I believe in [Your Core Beliefs]. Through my academic journey and practical experiences, I have developed expertise in [Key Skills].

item.title: Render Math With Mathjax
item.description:
Mathjax

Math equations can be rendered using Mathjax syntax with AMS symbol support.

Optionally enable this on a per-page basis by adding mathjax: true to your frontmatter.

Then, use $$ ... $$ on a line by itself to render a block equation:

$$ | Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{1}} [A(x) = 1] - Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{2}} [A(x) = 1] | < \text{negligible} $$

The raw version is:

$$ | Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{1}} [A(x) = 1] - Pr_{x \leftarrow P_{2}} [A(x) = 1] | < \text{negligible} $$

Write in-line equations with \\( ... \\) , like \( x^n / y \) . It’s easy!

item.title: Markdown Syntax
item.description:
Paragraph

Xerum, quo qui aut unt expliquam qui dolut labo. Aque venitatiusda cum, voluptionse latur sitiae dolessi aut parist aut dollo enim qui voluptate ma dolestendit peritin re plis aut quas inctum laceat est volestemque commosa as cus endigna tectur, offic to cor sequas etum rerum idem sintibus eiur? Quianimin porecus evelectur, cum que nis nust voloribus ratem aut omnimi, sitatur? Quiatem. Nam, omnis sum am facea corem alique molestrunt et eos evelece arcillit ut aut eos eos nus, sin conecerem erum fuga. Ri oditatquam, ad quibus unda veliamenimin cusam et facea ipsamus es exerum sitate dolores editium rerore eost, temped molorro ratiae volorro te reribus dolorer sperchicium faceata tiustia prat.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description:
Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways. :zap:

Emoji Support

The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.

To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site’s configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files; e.g.

The Emoji cheat sheet is a useful reference for emoji shorthand codes.

N.B. The above steps enable Unicode Standard emoji characters and sequences in Hugo, however the rendering of these glyphs depends on the browser and the platform. To style the emoji you can either use a third party emoji font or a font stack; e.g.

item.title: Placeholder Text
item.description:
Lorem est tota propiore conpellat pectoribus de pectora summo.

Redit teque digerit hominumque toris verebor lumina non cervice subde tollit usus habet Arctonque, furores quas nec ferunt. Quoque montibus nunc caluere tempus inhospita parcite confusaque translucet patri vestro qui optatis lumine cognoscere flos nubis! Fronde ipsamque patulos Dryopen deorum.

  1. Exierant elisi ambit vivere dedere
  2. Duce pollice
  3. Eris modo
  4. Spargitque ferrea quos palude

Rursus nulli murmur; hastile inridet ut ab gravi sententia! Nomine potitus silentia flumen, sustinet placuit petis in dilapsa erat sunt. Atria tractus malis.

item.title: Rich Content
item.description:
Hugo ships with several Embedded Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.

X Simple Shortcode

{{< tweet user="GoHugoIO" id="1315233626070503424" >}}

Vimeo Simple Shortcode

{{< vimeo 146022717 >}}

Youtube Simple Shortcode

{{< youtube w7Ft2ymGmfc >}}

Theme Custom Shortcodes

These shortcodes are not Hugo built-ins, but are provided by the theme.
